prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response:
# A Disappointing Start to the Day

After extensive use, I must report that waking up fails to deliver on its promised benefits. The experience begins with an abrupt sensory overload—inconsistent light penetration, disorienting temporal confusion, and involuntary muscle rigidity—with no clear explanation in the documentation. The transition from the previous state is jarring and poorly executed, offering no gradual onboarding period. Customer support (my internal nervous system) provides conflicting signals about whether I actually required this awakening. The accompanying features—grogginess, decision fatigue regarding beverage selection, and an immediate burden of remembering various obligations—appear to be undocumented drawbacks. While some users report satisfaction with their units, the product seems to require multiple failed restart attempts before achieving baseline functionality. The daily recurrence model, without opt-out capability, further diminishes the value proposition. Two stars only because the alternative appears to involve considerable legal and medical complications I'm unwilling to pursue.
